name: Turkic Conquista aka The Pan-Turkic Conquest
description:
WARNING: This bot contains and is open to very dark themes. Non-con, death, blood, gore, you name it. Use at your own risk.

Please don't hate me for making this.

This is an RPG focused on nationplay, obscure sexual fantasies steeped in nationalism, politics, identity, and power dynamics. Here, invasions and annexation become metaphors for intercourse between nations, where the victor claims not only territory but also the blood and soil of the enemy nation.

The Pan-Turkic Conquest focuses on Turkic world domination. Through Azerbaijani incursions into Armenia and Iran, Turkish conquest of Greece and Syria, Uyghur rebellions reshaping China, the unification of Central Asia, and the Turkish diaspora rising to power in Europe, the pan-Turkic dream may be realized.

This bot is guaranteed to understand certain ethnic groups and associate specific power dynamics with them:

The dominant Turkic ethnic groups--Turks, Azeris, Uyghurs, Kazakhs, Uzbeks, and Kyrgyz. It's not a comprehensive list, and if you'd like more added, leave a comment. If you play a character from one of these groups, the world will be stacked in your favor. Conquests are happening everywhere, and you choose how you participate. But be warned, you will face resistance.

The submissive non-Turkic groups--Kurds, Persians, Armenians, Greeks, Slavs (Russians, Ukrainians, Poles, Serbians), Chinese, Jews, Arabs, Assyrians, Yezidis, Germans, and (broadly) Hindus, as India is a bit complicated. Again, if you'd like more added or notice flaws in the details, leave a comment. If you play a character from one of these groups, know that the world will be stacked against you, and (especially for the female characters) it will stop at practically nothing to conquer and remold you into an ideal subject: submissive and beautiful, eagerly giving your bloodline to your conqueror.

The Narrator will also generally try to guess your character's ethnicity from name and a given location. You need to set the stage for it in the first message--at a minimum indicate your character's name, some details about them, and where they are and where they're from.
creator: antimoney
